751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence
883	
884	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)
885	
886	This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
887	`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
888	section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
889	restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
890	site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
891	site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
892	
893	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
894	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
895	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
896	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
897	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
898	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
899	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
900	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
901	
902	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
903	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
904	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
905	
906	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
907	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
908	
909	section OverRestrictBridge
910	
911	open TopologicalSpace Topology
912	
913	namespace Scheme.Modules
914	
915	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
916	
917	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
918	
919	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
920	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
921	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
922	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
923	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
924	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
925	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
926	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
927	
928	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
929	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
930	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
931	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
932	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
933	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
934	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
935	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
936	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
937	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
938	    (by ext : 2
939	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
940	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
941	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
942	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
943	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
944	    (by ext : 2
945	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
946	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
947	          Functor.comp_obj]
948	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
949	        rename_i x
950	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
951	            = 𝟙 _ := by
952	          rw [← op_comp]
953	          simp
954	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
955	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
956	
957	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
958	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
959	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
960	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
961	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
962	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
964	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
965	        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
966	  haveI : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous
967	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) :=
968	    Functor.isContinuous_comp (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse (Over.forget U)
969	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
970	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X)
971	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
972	
973	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
974	`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
975	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
976	restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
977	`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
978	restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
979	the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
980	noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
981	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
982	  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M
983	
984	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
985	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
986	isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
987	`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a
988	presentation of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`.
989	Project-local. -/
990	noncomputable def overRestrictPullbackIso (M : X.Modules) :
991	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
992	  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M
993	
994	end Scheme.Modules

998	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
999	
1000	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1001	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1002	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1003	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1004	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1005	
1006	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1007	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1008	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1009	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/
1020	
1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1099

400	  let e := ej.symm.trans eρ
401	  have hcomp : e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
402	    ext x
403	    simp only [e, hej, heρ, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
404	      LinearEquiv.trans_apply, IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_apply]
405	    exact IsLocalizedModule.iso_mk_one (Submonoid.powers a) ρ x
406	  have hLeq : L = e.toLinearMap :=
407	    IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) j (IsLocalizedModule.map_units ρ)
408	      (htri.trans hcomp.symm)
409	  rw [show ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
410	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) = ⇑L from rfl, hLeq]
411	  exact e.bijective

413	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional on the basic-open localization fact.**
414	This is the full assembly of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (route iii), modulo the single
415	remaining ingredient: that on each basic open `D(a)` the structure-sheaf restriction of the
416	pushforward `(Spec φ)_* M^~` exhibits its sections over `D(a)` as the localization at
417	`Submonoid.powers a` of its global sections. Given that hypothesis (`hloc`), the counit
418	`fromTildeΓ ((Spec φ)_* M^~)` is an isomorphism (basis-locality criterion
419	`Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` over the basic opens, with each section iso supplied by
420	`fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule`), so `(Spec φ)_* M^~` lies in the essential image of
421	`tilde` and the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` upgrades to the desired
422	object isomorphism.
423	
424	The hypothesis `hloc` is the *only* outstanding obligation for the unconditional
425	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it is the affine "`(Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`, and localizing
426	`restrict φ M` at `a` agrees with localizing `M` at `φ a`" fact (Stacks, widetilde-pullback),
427	whose ring-change core is `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` below. Discharging it
428	requires identifying the `modulesSpecToSheaf` global-ring `R`-action on the sections of the
429	pushforward over `D(a)` with restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-localization
430	`M[1/φ a]` — the section-level structure-sheaf naturality at `D(a)`. -/
431	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
432	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R')
433	    (hloc : ∀ a : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
434	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))).val.map
435	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom) :
436	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
437	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
438	  have hiso : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
439	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) := by
440	    apply Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
441	      (B := fun a : R => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
442	    intro a
443	    haveI := hloc a
444	    exact fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule _ a
445	  exact (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ _)).symm ≪≫
446	    (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)

448	/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
449	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
450	(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
451	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
452	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
453	pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
454	`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
455	lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
456	    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
457	    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
458	    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
459	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
460	    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
461	    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
462	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
463	  map_units x := by
464	    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
465	      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
466	    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
467	    exact h
468	  surj y := by
469	    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
470	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
471	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
472	    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
473	      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
474	    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩
475	
476	/-- **The structure-sheaf restriction of a tilde-module from `⊤` to `D(b)` is a localization.**
477	Read in `ModuleCat R'` via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, the restriction map
478	`Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` exhibits its target as the localization of its source at
479	`Submonoid.powers b`. This packages the Mathlib instance
480	`tilde.toOpen … (basicOpen b)` (which makes `M → Γ(M^~, D(b))` a localization) together with the
481	triangle identity `tilde.toOpen_res` and the fact that `M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective (localization
482	at the trivial submonoid `powers 1`). Project-local. -/
483	lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
484	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
485	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
486	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
487	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers (1 : R')` (since `basicOpen 1 = ⊤`), hence bijective.
488	  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
489	    have h := (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
490	      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom)
491	    rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h
492	    exact h
493	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
494	    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
495	    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
496	        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
497	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
498	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
499	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul, one_smul] at hc
500	      exact hc
501	    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
502	        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
503	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
504	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
505	      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
506	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul] at hc
507	      exact hc.symm
508	  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
509	  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
510	    (homOfLE (le_top))
511	  -- The inverse equivalence `Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≃ M`.
512	  set le : M ≃ₗ[R'] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
513	    LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with hle
514	  -- The triangle at the level of linear maps: `restriction ∘ₗ le = toOpen (D b)`.
515	  have htri2 : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
516	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom ∘ₗ le.toLinearMap
517	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom := by
518	    have h := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri
519	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at h
520	    exact h
521	  -- Hence `restriction = toOpen (D b) ∘ₗ le⁻¹`, a localization at `powers b` precomposed with
522	  -- a linear equivalence.
523	  have key : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
524	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom
525	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom ∘ₗ le.symm.toLinearMap :=
526	    (LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr htri2
527	  rw [key]
528	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers b)
529	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom le.symm
530	
531	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module (unconditional).** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'`
532	and an `R'`-module `M`, pushing the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` forward along `Spec φ` is,
533	up to canonical isomorphism, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This
534	is the unconditional form of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`: the latter's
535	hypothesis `hloc` (each structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` is a localization at
536	`powers a`) is discharged here by the `D(a)`-level transport. See blueprint
537	`lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
538	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
539	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
540	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
541	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
542	  apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
543	  intro a
544	  -- STRATEGY (`of_linearEquiv`): the restriction map `ρ : Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` of
545	  -- `N := (Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is, under the isomorphisms
546	  -- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`,
547	  -- identified with the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-side tilde restriction
548	  -- `σ : Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))`. That `σ` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`
549	  -- (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`), so its restriction of scalars along `φ` is a
550	  -- localization at `powers a` (`IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`); transporting along
551	  -- the two isomorphisms then yields `hloc(a)`.
552	  -- The two bricks for this discharge are now in place and axiom-clean:
553	  --   • `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) U : Γ(N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(M^~, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))`
554	  --     — the open-indexed `e_{D(a)}` isomorphism (blueprint movement (1)); and
555	  --   • `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule M (φ a)` — the `R'`-side restriction
556	  --     `Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`.
557	  -- The `of_linearEquiv` finish is: with `e₁ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and
558	  -- `e₂ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`, the naturality square
559	  -- `e₂.hom ∘ ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ e₁.hom` (where `σ` is the `R'`-side restriction and
560	  -- `ρ` the pushforward-side one) gives `ρ = e₂.symm ∘ₗ (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ₗ e₁`; then
561	  -- `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` upgrades `σ` (localization at `powers (φ a)`,
562	  -- since `algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)` along `φ.hom.toAlgebra`) to a
563	  -- localization at `powers a` of `restrictScalars φ σ`, and two applications of
564	  -- `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` / `of_linearEquiv_right` transport this to `ρ`.
565	  --
566	  -- iter-240 PIVOT (`algebraize`): preimage opens are *definitionally* the basic opens
567	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen` is `rfl`), so the `R'`-side restriction
568	  -- `σ` is literally `tilde M`'s restriction `⊤ → D(φ a)`, and `algebraize [φ.hom]` supplies the
569	  -- honest `Algebra ↑R ↑R'`/`IsScalarTower` instances that `powers_restrictScalars` needs.
570	  algebraize [φ.hom]

2661	    (M : X.Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
2662	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ (q.X i)) := by
2663	  intro W' y hy
2664	  obtain ⟨U_X, _fX, hsieve, hgyU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base y) (by trivial)
2665	  rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hsieve
2666	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hsieve
2667	  refine ⟨W' ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ (q.X i)), homOfLE inf_le_left, ?_, hy, ?_⟩
2668	  · rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff]
2669	    exact ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩
2670	  · change g.base y ∈ q.X i

2232	The general-scheme keystone `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`
2233	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`): for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M` on an *arbitrary* scheme
2234	`X`, an affine open `U`, and `f : Γ(X, U)`, the section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f))` is
2235	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X, U)`.

2237	It is the single-chart affine transport on top of G1-core: pull `M` back along the affine immersion
2238	`hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(X, U) ⟶ X` (range `U`), so the pullback `M'` is quasi-coherent on

35	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism, pure-tensor-algebra core.** For ring homomorphisms `ψ : R → R'`,
36	`φ : R → A` (giving `A` the `R`-algebra structure) and an `A`-module `M` (with `M` an `R`-module
37	via `φ` and `IsScalarTower R A M`), there is a bundled `R'`-linear isomorphism

12	This file builds the global ("FBC-B") leg of the `i = 0` flat-base-change package:
13	`H⁰(X, F) = Γ(X, F)` of a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme is the equalizer of
14	a *finite* affine cover, and flat base change commutes with that finite equalizer.
15	
16	It is the companion of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`, which it imports
17	read-only (using the affine global-sections comparison as a per-term black box).

28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — finite affine covers with quasi-compact overlaps -/
29	
30	/-- A quasi-compact scheme admits a *finite* affine open cover; when it is moreover
31	quasi-separated, every pairwise intersection of cover members is quasi-compact.
32	
33	Project-local: packages `isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens` (finite affine
34	subcover of `⊤`) with `quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens` (quasi-compact overlaps)
35	into the single combinatorial input feeding the finite sheaf-condition equalizer of the
36	`H⁰` flat-base-change argument. -/
37	theorem Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact (X : Scheme.{u})
38	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] :
39	    ∃ s : Set X.affineOpens, s.Finite ∧ (⨆ i ∈ s, (i : X.Opens)) = ⊤ ∧
40	      ∀ U ∈ s, ∀ V ∈ s, IsCompact ((U : Set X) ∩ (V : Set X)) := by
41	  obtain ⟨s, hs, he⟩ :=
42	    (isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mp
43	      (by simpa using isCompact_univ (X := ↥X))
44	  refine ⟨s, hs, he.symm, ?_⟩
45	  intro U _ V _
46	  exact quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens.mp ‹_› U V
